page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.cm4.6xlarge is a KVM-based virtual machine instance configured with 24 dedicated vCPUs and 96.0 GB of system memory. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8369HB processor running at 3.2 GHz, this x86_64 architecture server allocates one physical core per vCPU with single-thread execution. The processor features a 33 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512, AVX-512_VNNI, and AVX-512_BF16, which accelerate vector calculations and machine learning operations. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPU hardware, relying on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 10 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the ecs.cm4.6xlarge demonstrates balanced performance across compute and memory operations. The stress-ng single-core CPU test yielded a score of 1575.18, while the multi-core test reached 24949.83. Memory performance is characterized by a read-only small-block cached bandwidth of 1392.19 GB/sec, which drops to 96.09 GB/sec for large-block uncached reads, and a write bandwidth of 78.54 GB/sec. The system achieved a PassMark CPU score of 24266.27 and handled 23.45 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks. For lightweight AI workloads, the CPU-based LLM inference speed for a medium 7B model reached 35.12 tokens per second for prompt processing and 11.24 tokens per second for text generation.

The instance offers a balanced resource density with a 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, providing a stable cost-to-performance profile for workloads requiring dedicated CPU allocation without the overhead of hyperthreading. The lack of bundled local storage requires users to factor in network storage performance and cost. This configuration is suitable for general-purpose workloads, static web serving (benchmarked at 14.7 GB/sec throughput), database operations, and multi-threaded data decompression tasks.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.cm4.6xlarge is a KVM-based instance featuring 24 dedicated Intel Xeon 8369HB vCPUs running at 3.2 GHz and 96.0 GB of RAM. Utilizing an x86_64 architecture with a 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, it delivers dedicated compute power without hyperthreading. The processor includes a 33 MB L3 cache and supports AVX-512 vector instructions. With no local storage or GPUs, the instance relies on a 10 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results show solid multi-core performance with a stress-ng score of 24949.83 and a PassMark CPU score of 24266.27. It also demonstrates strong database capabilities, processing 23.45 million Redis SET operations per second and achieving 14.7 GB/sec in static web serving throughput. This instance is optimized for general-purpose applications, database operations, web serving, and moderate multi-threaded data processing workloads.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.cm4.6xlarge is a KVM instance with 24 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 96 GB RAM, and a 10 Gbps network baseline, optimized for general-purpose and database workloads.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.cm4.6xlarge features 24 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 96 GB RAM, delivering balanced compute and memory performance.
tagline: A dedicated Intel Xeon instance offering balanced compute and memory resources for enterprise databases and web serving workloads.
bullet_points: 24 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs running at 3.2 GHz
96.0 GB of RAM providing a balanced 1:4 resource ratio
AVX-512 instruction support for accelerated vector calculations
10 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Proven performance in database operations and static web serving
